surface.name: W&B Models Quickstart through a first tracked training run
surface.selection_basis: The official W&B Quickstart is the general Models getting-started route and demonstrates a Python run that logs accuracy and loss for display in W&B. Its signup link, however, resolves to a client-rendered login application whose public response does not enumerate registration inputs or transitions.

alternatives_considered.name: W&B Weave Python SDK quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Models quickstart explicitly redirects developers seeking Weave to this separate product surface.

alternatives_considered.name: Service account API key
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The official personal-key tab is the direct individual-developer route; service accounts require team or organization settings.

alternatives_considered.name: Python notebook route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a peer execution environment for the same experiment; the Python script route is fully shown without a notebook dependency.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a W&B account and run the official Python experiment until accuracy and loss are recorded for viewing in W&B.
assumed_prior_state: No W&B account, API key, project, run, local wandb package, or logged metric is assumed.
Python and pip are available locally; no account, key, package installation, login, run, or metric was created during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established by public official documentation
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: No complete W&B first-success route can be established because the current account-registration form and transitions are not publicly enumerated.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The public login response contains an empty application root and scripts, not a rendered signup form or documented post-submit state.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The required account-registration transition is not established by public official documentation.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quickstart later demonstrates recorded accuracy and loss in the W&B App, but the path from no account cannot be reconstructed without inventing hidden signup inputs and transitions.

prerequisites.requirement: A W&B user account is required before the personal API-key steps; the current public registration form is not enumerated.
Create a named personal API key in User Settings and copy it at its one-time full display.
Have Python and pip to install and run the wandb library.

primary_path.action: Open W&B Quickstart and follow its Sign Up link.
primary_path.output: The W&B account application is requested.
primary_path.success_signal: The official signup URL is reached.

primary_path.action: Redirect the app.wandb.ai signup URL to the client-rendered wandb.ai login application.
primary_path.output: The public response exposes an empty `root` element and client scripts.
primary_path.success_signal: No signup fields, validation states, or registration completion transition are publicly inspectable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The Quickstart begins its key instructions with an already authenticated user and does not document account creation.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The signup application does not expose a public form and the Quickstart assumes an authenticated user. The documented continuation is User Settings, Create new API key, name, Create, one-time copy, set WANDB_API_KEY, install wandb, log in, run the published training script, and visit wandb.ai/home to view recorded accuracy and loss.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The documented post-account experiment cannot form an end-to-end first-user candidate without hidden account inputs and transitions.

branches.path: Open team or organization Service Accounts, select the account action menu, create and name a key, copy it once, and select Done.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds team or organization access and replaces the personal key owner.

branches.condition: Use Python or a notebook for login
branches.path: Install wandb and call `wandb.login()` instead of exporting WANDB_API_KEY and running `wandb login`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes authentication interface but converges on the same training script.

friction_gates.description: The required registration interface is client-rendered and its complete form and transitions are absent from public official documentation.
The full W&B API key is shown only once; if lost, the documented recovery is to create a new key.

time_to_first_success.value: The Quickstart says developers can install W&B and start tracking, visualizing, and managing experiments in minutes, but gives no signup-to-recorded-metrics duration.

sources.title: W&B Quickstart
sections_used: Sign up and create an API key
Install the wandb library and log in
Initialize a run and track hyperparameters
Create a machine learning training experiment

sources.title: login
evidence_supported: Empty application root without rendered signup form

uncertainties.question: What exact authentication choices, fields, terms, verification actions, and post-submit states appear in current W&B signup?
uncertainties.impact: The account portion of the first mile cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart assumes login and the public application response contains no rendered registration form.

excluded_after_success: No complete first-success boundary is claimed while account registration remains undocumented.
Reports, Artifacts, Sweeps, Tables, integrations, and Weave occur after the documented first tracked experiment.
